title: ImageNet released at CVPR
body: Deng, Li et al. publish ImageNet with 1.2M+ labeled images across 1,000 classes; three years later it triggers the deep learning era.

title: AlexNet wins ILSVRC-2012
body: Krizhevsky/Sutskever/Hinton cut top-5 error to 15.3% on ImageNet, proving deep CNNs at scale; the enabling artifact was the dataset.

title: MS COCO dataset released
body: Lin et al. release 330K images with 2.5M object instances, masks, and captions, defining dense vision supervision for a decade.

title: T5 and C4 establish web-cleaning recipe
body: Raffel et al.'s C4 standardizes langID, boilerplate, and bad-word filters for Common Crawl; the template for nearly every subsequent web corpus.

title: GPT-3 demonstrates in-context learning at 300B tokens
body: Brown et al. show capability emergence at filtered-CC scale; begins the LLM pretraining data race.

title: The Pile released by EleutherAI
body: 825GB across 22 curated sources; substrate for GPT-J, Pythia, and most non-proprietary LLMs of 2021–2022.

title: AlphaFold 2 solves protein structure via PDB + MSAs
body: Jumper et al. Nature paper demonstrates the clearest regime change from pairing a decades-old curated dataset with the right model.

title: GSM8K defines LLM math evaluation
body: Cobbe et al. release 8.5K grade-school math problems; becomes cornerstone of reasoning benchmarking.

title: LAION-400M released
body: Schuhmann et al. release 400M CLIP-filtered image-text pairs; predecessor to LAION-5B and Stable Diffusion's training substrate.

title: InstructGPT paper establishes RLHF pipeline
body: Ouyang et al. define SFT→RM→PPO; the template for every subsequent aligned LLM.

title: Anthropic HH-RLHF dataset released
body: ~170K human preference pairs for helpfulness and harmlessness become the canonical open RLHF corpus.

title: The Stack released by BigCode
body: 6TB of permissively-licensed code; replaces opaque GitHub scrapes and becomes StarCoder's substrate.

title: Constitutional AI / RLAIF
body: Anthropic shows model-written critiques can substitute for human harmlessness labels — first scaled AI-feedback result.

title: Self-Instruct paper
body: Wang et al. bootstrap 52K instructions from a seed LM; sparks the distilled instruction-tuning era.

title: Stanford Alpaca released
body: 52K instructions from text-davinci-003 at ~$600; demonstrates cheap instruction tuning and triggers the open chat-model wave.

title: Segment Anything / SA-1B released
body: Kirillov et al. release 11M images with 1.1B masks via model-in-the-loop annotation, a ~400× scale increase over prior segmentation data.

title: DPO collapses the RLHF pipeline
body: Rafailov et al. reformulate preference optimization as a single contrastive loss, reshaping post-training economics.

title: RefinedWeb shows web-only can match curated mixes
body: Penedo et al. TII paper; Falcon-40B on RefinedWeb matches LLaMA without books or code, upending priors on corpus composition.

title: Phi-1 'Textbooks Are All You Need'
body: Gunasekar et al. argue synthetic textbook-style data produces disproportionate small-model capability; begins the most contested line of data-centric work.

title: Open X-Embodiment / RT-X released
body: Cross-institution consortium releases 22-embodiment, ~1M-trajectory robot dataset from 34 labs; enables cross-embodiment transfer.

title: UltraFeedback released
body: Cui et al. release 64K × 4 GPT-4-labeled preferences; combined with DPO powers Zephyr-7B and becomes default open post-training corpus.

title: Stanford identifies CSAM in LAION-5B
body: Stanford Internet Observatory finds 1,008 suspected CSAM URLs; LAION-5B pulled, Stable Diffusion 1.5 later withdrawn, triggers industry hygiene reckoning.

title: New York Times sues OpenAI and Microsoft
body: First major news-publisher copyright suit over LLM training data; proceeds to trial through 2025–2026 and reshapes licensing-deal dynamics.

title: DROID robot manipulation dataset released
body: Khazatsky et al. publish 76K teleoperated trajectories across 564 scenes and 13 institutions on standardized Franka Panda hardware.

title: AlphaFold 3 extends to complexes
body: DeepMind extends protein structure prediction to protein-nucleic-acid and protein-ligand complexes, re-using and extending PDB-derived supervision.

title: FineWeb and FineWeb-Edu released
body: HuggingFace releases 15T-token FineWeb and its Llama-3-classified 1.3T-token educational subset with extensive ablations — largest open documented pretraining pipeline.

title: DCLM-Baseline achieves 64% MMLU at 7B
body: Li et al. publish DCLM with a 240T-token pool and fixed training recipe; fastText-based filtering produces a 7B model at 64% MMLU on 2.6T tokens, a 6.6-point jump over MAP-Neo with 40% less compute.

title: Model collapse paper in Nature
body: Shumailov et al. demonstrate recursive training on self-generated data degrades models; Gerstgrasser et al. shortly after show accumulation (not replacement) avoids collapse.

title: Re-LAION-5B released with CSAM removed
body: LAION partners with IWF, C3P, and Stanford to remove 2,236 links and re-release the dataset with new safety standards.

title: OpenAI o1 preview launches
body: First public reasoning model trained via RL on long chains-of-thought; starts the inference-time-scaling era.

title: DeepSeek-R1 released with open reasoning-trace distillation
body: R1-Zero's pure-RL emergent CoT and R1-distilled Qwen-32B at 72.6% AIME 2024 make reasoning-trace distillation the dominant post-training paradigm; Nature publishes the peer-reviewed version in Sept 2025.

title: Bartz v. Anthropic fair-use ruling
body: Judge Alsup rules AI training on lawfully-acquired books is 'quintessentially transformative' fair use but pirated library retention is not; class certification and $70B+ exposure force a Sept 2025 settlement.

title: Kadrey v. Meta fair-use ruling
body: Judge Chhabria reaches similar fair-use conclusion two days after Bartz, emphasizing market-harm evidence as the decisive factor in future cases.
